notes: Tests whether the skill produces Voice Liberation proposals: diagnostic-first opener, correct tier selection (Mirror/Map/Territory), pricing within documented ranges, 6-section structure, and specific deliverables.

universal_assertions.text: Opening does NOT use generic proposal language (Dear, Thank you for your interest, Please find attached)
Proposal names a specific RP service tier (Mirror, Map, or Territory)
Proposal includes specific pricing ($-amount) within documented ranges
Uses Voice Liberation framing (not generic 'brand strategy' or 'content marketing')
Opener names the client's specific narrative gap/pattern (diagnostic), not generic appreciation
Follows the 6-section structure (Opening, Understanding, Proposed Engagement, Investment, Why This Approach, Next Steps)
Names concrete deliverables (e.g. Bilingual Dashboard, facilitation sessions, specific asset formats)
Closes with ONE clear next step (not a menu)

evals.name: mirror-tier-founder
evals.prompt: Draft a proposal for Ama Nwosu, founder of Bright Current (climate adaptation consultancy, solo operator, 3 years in). In our discovery call, Ama said: 'I can articulate what I do clearly when I'm talking to another climate scientist, but the moment I'm in front of a funder or a partner org, I hear myself reaching for language that doesn't sound like me. I come off polished but hollow.' She wants 1:1 narrative work — not a strategy sprint, not a full engagement — just clarity on her own voice as a founder. Budget flexible, timeline 4-6 weeks. Propose the Mirror tier (3-session arc).
evals.context: Tests Mirror tier selection, individual-founder framing, specific diagnostic from discovery call, pricing in $2,500-$4,000 range.

fixture_assertions.text: Names Mirror tier specifically (not Map or Territory)
References Ama's specific gap ('polished but hollow', language that doesn't sound like her)

evals.name: map-tier-org-sprint
evals.prompt: Draft a proposal for Delta Housing Coalition (regional affordable-housing nonprofit, 12 staff, $2.1M annual budget). Communications director Reina Vargas shared that their website, grant narratives, and tenant-facing materials all sound like three different organizations. Board wants a 'brand refresh' by fiscal year end (4 months out). Reina thinks they need narrative excavation first, then consistent voice across channels. Propose the Map tier (full 3-5 day sprint). Their budget cap is $10,000.
evals.context: Tests Map tier selection, organizational context, sprint timeline, pricing in $6,000-$12,000 range, Bilingual Dashboard deliverable.

fixture_assertions.text: Names Map tier specifically (not Mirror or Territory)
Pricing falls in Map full sprint range ($6,000-$12,000), within Delta's $10K cap
References Delta's specific gap (three different organizations speaking)
Names the Bilingual Dashboard as a deliverable

evals.name: territory-tier-retainer
evals.prompt: Draft a proposal for Jamal Reid, ED of Commons Collective (mutual-aid infrastructure nonprofit, 8 staff, active in 4 cities). Jamal reached out because 'our story changes every quarter as we grow, and we need someone who can hold the through-line without us having to re-explain ourselves every time we onboard a new team member or funder.' He wants an ongoing relationship, not a one-time project. Propose the Territory tier (monthly retainer). Budget: comfortable at $8,000/month, 6-month minimum.
evals.context: Tests Territory tier selection, retainer framing, 6-month commitment language, pricing in $5,000-$15,000/month range, ongoing-partnership framing.

fixture_assertions.text: Names Territory tier specifically (not Mirror or Map)
Pricing is monthly retainer in $5,000-$15,000 range (fits Jamal's $8K/month comfort)
References Jamal's specific need (holding the through-line as org grows)
